cellular_response.notes: Intracellular-to-canalicular export is mass-conserving. Surface activity is a relative experimental input, not a turnover rate. CYP7A1 feedback and basolateral escape remain absent because the evidence panel does not identify transferable absolute rates. Damage exposure is stress-time (s), not a lesion count. Fate is evidence-only until a matched temporal commitment calibration is supplied.

Total tissue ADP is not compartment resolved.
Total tissue AMP is not compartment resolved.
Tissue-average energy charge does not resolve hepatocyte zones or organelle compartments.

Magnetization-transfer exchange does not identify net mitochondrial ATP synthesis, cellular ATP demand, or first-order ADP/ATP rate constants.

atp_death_switch.notes: The established apoptosis module owns irreversible apoptosis-versus-necrosis commitment.
atp_death_switch.title: Intracellular ATP is a switch in the decision between apoptosis and necrosis

autoencoding_variational_bayes.notes: Original reparameterized variational lower-bound framework underlying VAEs.
autoencoding_variational_bayes.title: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes

bi2006_human_schh_taurocholate_transport.notes: Five-lot sandwich-cultured human-hepatocyte taurocholate apparent uptake, intrinsic biliary clearance and BEI ranges. These are coupled system outputs, not individual transporter rates.
bi2006_human_schh_taurocholate_transport.title: Use of cryopreserved human hepatocytes in sandwich culture to measure hepatobiliary transport

bile_acid_mitochondrial_apoptosis.notes: Hydrophobic bile-acid exposure in freshly isolated rat hepatocytes caused ROS, mitochondrial permeability transition, and apoptosis.
bile_acid_mitochondrial_apoptosis.title: Bile acid-induced rat hepatocyte apoptosis is inhibited by antioxidants and MPT blockers

brian2_2_10_1.notes: Audited optional backend version; Brian2 supplies equation/event execution, not biological equations or parameter values.
brian2_2_10_1.title: Brian2 2.10.1

brian2_custom_events.notes: Documents event-triggered interactions suitable for an optional communication backend.
brian2_custom_events.title: Brian2 2.10.1: custom events

bsep_cholestasis.notes: Loss of BSEP function changes hepatocyte bile-acid disposition and sensitizes to cholestatic injury.
bsep_cholestasis.title: Disruption of BSEP Function in HepaRG Cells Alters Bile Acid Disposition

cajulao2022_gcgr_endocytosis.notes: Supports agonist-dependent clathrin/dynamin GCGR internalization and endosomal signaling. Glucagon is modeled as extracellular exposure, not as a cell-scale projectile.
cajulao2022_gcgr_endocytosis.title: Glucagon receptor-mediated regulation of gluconeogenic gene transcription is endocytosis-dependent in primary hepatocytes

cholestasis_er_stress.notes: Reduced bile-acid transporter expression in hepatocytes produces intrahepatic cholestasis with ER stress.
cholestasis_er_stress.title: Hepatocyte-specific ablation of Foxa2 alters bile acid homeostasis and results in ER stress

choudhary2014_vdac_transport.notes: Supports VDAC as the principal outer-mitochondrial-membrane pathway for ATP/ADP and other respiratory substrates. Simulation-derived transport energetics are not transferred to PHH.
choudhary2014_vdac_transport.title: ATP transport through VDAC and the VDAC-tubulin complex probed by equilibrium and nonequilibrium MD simulations

cimadamore2023_slc25a4_exchange.notes: Purified human SLC25A4 establishes equimolar ADP/ATP exchange topology. Its assay kinetics are not transferred to a hepatocyte.
cimadamore2023_slc25a4_exchange.title: Human mitochondrial ADP/ATP carrier SLC25A4 operates with a ping-pong kinetic mechanism

duarte1989_human_hepatocyte_volume.notes: Normal-human in-situ intermediate-zone stereology reports mean hepatocyte volume 2850 +/- 99.9 um3 across five selected cases.
duarte1989_human_hepatocyte_volume.title: Baseline volume data of human liver parenchymal cell

evans1976_human_membrane_area_lysis.notes: Human red-cell micropipette measurements reported 2-4% maximum fractional area expansion before lysis (3% mean). This is not a hepatocyte-specific failure measurement; the runtime uses half the lower bound as a conservative engineering cap.
evans1976_human_membrane_area_lysis.title: Elastic area compressibility modulus of red cell membrane

expression_unknown_policy.notes: Gene-specific RNA and protein kinetics remain null until measured or calibrated; generic demo rates cannot silently become hepatocyte parameters.
expression_unknown_policy.title: Genome-expression milestone missing-data policy

fabyan2026_human_liver_3d.notes: Human cleared-tissue 3D reconstruction supports tissue-scale spatial architecture. It does not provide a donor-general hepatocyte mechanics law or justify the canonical polyhedron used by this runtime.
fabyan2026_human_liver_3d.title: 3D reconstruction of human liver tissue at cellular resolution

fabyan2026_liver_3d.notes: Open human 3D liver-tissue reconstruction; lobule and central-vein morphometry constrain future tissue geometry, not one hepatocyte.
fabyan2026_liver_3d.title: 3D reconstruction of human liver tissue at cellular resolution

fujiwara2002_phospholipid_hop_diffusion.notes: Single-molecule tracking in NRK cells measured rapid DOPE motion inside approximately 230-nm compartments and slower macroscopic hop diffusion imposed by the actin-based membrane skeleton. These values are not transferred to human hepatocytes.
fujiwara2002_phospholipid_hop_diffusion.title: Phospholipids undergo hop diffusion in compartmentalized cell membrane

genomic_burst_kinetics.notes: Mammalian alleles transcribe in bursts; enhancer and core-promoter architecture affect burst frequency and size.
genomic_burst_kinetics.title: Genomic encoding of transcriptional burst kinetics

geo_gse289636.notes: Primary repository accession registered by the Peng et al. study for six PHH batches.
geo_gse289636.title: GEO Series GSE289636: primary human hepatocyte single-cell RNA-seq

glucagon_creb_hepatic_control.notes: Supports the hepatic glucagon/cAMP/PKA/CREB-to-gluconeogenic-program direction; no project kinetic constants are inferred.
glucagon_creb_hepatic_control.title: CREB regulates hepatic gluconeogenesis through the coactivator PGC-1

glutathione_redox.notes: Cytosolic GSH/GSSG normally >100:1, maintained by GSSG reductase using NADPH; falls <4:1 under severe oxidative stress.
glutathione_redox.title: Role of glutathione redox status in liver injury (Am. J. Physiol. 2006)

grankvist2024_human_liver_fluxomics.notes: Primary human intact-liver-tissue study combining 13C tracing, spent-medium measurements, mass spectrometry and model-based flux analysis. It supports the required measurement strategy, not numeric transfer into PHH spheroids.
grankvist2024_human_liver_fluxomics.title: Global 13C tracing and metabolic flux analysis of intact human liver tissue ex vivo

gtex_v8.notes: Bulk tissue reference; liver values are not single-hepatocyte molecule counts and require cell-composition-aware interpretation.
gtex_v8.title: NIH Genotype-Tissue Expression project, data release V8

guillou2016_membrane_surface_reservoirs.notes: Micropipette aspiration shows that T lymphocytes can deform at constant volume while apparent surface area increases through membrane-reservoir unfolding. This supports only the kinematic constant-volume principle; no T-cell parameter is transferred to PHH.
guillou2016_membrane_surface_reservoirs.title: T-lymphocyte passive deformation is controlled by unfolding of membrane surface reservoirs

hcv_epigenetic_scar.notes: A defined exposure-specific persistent liver epigenetic scar; not a generic stress-memory rate.
hcv_epigenetic_scar.title: HCV-induced epigenetic changes persist after sustained virologic response

helfrich1973_bilayer_curvature.notes: Separates stretching, tilt, and curvature and supplies the curvature-energy framework for closed fluid bilayers. The project uses the model topology, not an inferred PHH bending modulus.
helfrich1973_bilayer_curvature.title: Elastic properties of lipid bilayers: theory and possible experiments

hepatocyte_connexin32_signal_propagation.notes: Mouse in-vivo evidence that hepatocyte Cx32 gap junctions support intercellular propagation of a glycogen-mobilizing signal.
hepatocyte_connexin32_signal_propagation.title: Defective propagation of signals generated by sympathetic nerve stimulation in connexin32-deficient liver

human_hepatocyte_renewal.notes: Retrospective carbon-14 birth dating; population renewal is not a fixed single-cell lifespan.

koenig2012_author_executable_reencoding.notes: Pinned author-maintained SBML Level 3 re-encoding with 36 kinetic laws and permissive redistribution terms embedded in the file; its source repository is MIT licensed. koenig_glucose_glycogen_protocol_script.notes: The repository script fixes external lactate at 0.8 mM, integrates for 200 minutes, and uses a nonuniform glycogen grid that does not contain 250 mM.

koenig_glucose_model_author_repository.notes: Pinned author repository used only for model-lineage and protocol auditing. No explicit repository license was found, so its legacy executable is not vendored.

legacy_atp_exchange_fixture_v1.notes: Software-only mapping from a whole-liver Pi-to-ATP exchange observation to matched ADP-to-ATP and ATP-to-ADP first-order reactions. The mapping is not a measured biological kinetic law.

legacy_redox_fixture_v1.notes: The four executable rate constants and starting concentrations are uncalibrated software-fixture values, not human PHH kinetics.

lewis2014_compartmental_nadph.notes: Isotope tracing resolves distinct cytosolic and mitochondrial NADPH pathways. Cancer-cell-line fluxes are not transferred to PHH.

libroadrunner_2_9_2.notes: Pinned optional runtime used only to regenerate the shadow-validation artifact.

lodish1983_hepg2_secretory_transit.notes: Pulse-chase evidence for selective ER-to-Golgi transit in human HepG2 hepatoma cells. It establishes pathway structure and system-dependent rates; it is not a numeric PHH calibration.

mallanna2016_phh_surfaceome.notes: Cell-surface capture identified 300 N-glycoproteins in primary human hepatocytes; density, domain and orientation were not measured.

peng2022_phh_requirements_standard.notes: Published CSCB standard source for ALB and HNF4A >=90 percent PHH product-marker criteria.

limitations: The challenge-medium initial volume, remaining-volume schedule and volumetric factor are not reported.
The reported endpoint is signed net medium glucose disappearance, not GLUT2 influx or intracellular pathway flux.
High-insulin conditions do not report a measured zero glucagon concentration.
The 13.4 s value is a whole-liver mean transit time, not one sinusoid residence time.
Boundary recovery is perfusion homeostasis; it does not claim hepatocyte glucose uptake.
No mouse spatial-metabolomics effect size is transferred into the human model.
No anatomical sinusoid control volume or human zonal pO2 is assigned.

glucose_open_system.policy: A physiological sinusoid and a finite PHH spheroid culture are non-interchangeable. Only exact model-generated signed outputs may be projected into the assay; missing volumes, transport rates and concentration trajectories remain null.

corrections_to_supplied_tables: High glucose is 11 mM, not 25 mM.
Low insulin is 0.1 nM (100 pM), not approximately 1 nM.
Low-insulin conditions contain 100 nM supplemented glucagon and are not no-hormone basal conditions.
The four highlighted uptake values are 0-6 h means with reported SD, not context-free point estimates.
High- versus low-insulin condition contrasts also change glucagon supplementation and cannot identify a pure insulin causal effect.
The delivered heldout-validation CSV contains model predictions and no numeric human held-out comparator.
The Wilson human scale bridge supports cells per gram of liver, not human single-hepatocyte volume or membrane geometry.

artifacts.reason: The claimed 1.2 mM lactate/default-250 mM protocol and universal 5/5 result conflict with the repository's four-way lineage audit. The current vendored executable remains 2/5; only the non-vendored legacy lineage reaches 5/5 under recovered 0.8 mM lactate and an actually selected 276.6667 mM glycogen grid point.
Primary Kemas 2021 article text was used to restore Table 1 time windows, SDs, exact media conditions and downstream insulin responses.

The supplied 25 mM high-glucose label is wrong (primary source: 11 mM), low insulin is 0.1 nM rather than about 1 nM, low-insulin conditions also contain 100 nM glucagon, and the supplied rows omitted SD and 0-6 h window semantics.
The primary source supports a matched insulin exposure and pAKT/PCK1/G6PC responses, but no INSR surface abundance, receptor occupancy, EC50, Hill coefficient or complete kinetic trajectory.
No numeric in-situ human sinusoidal pO2 or compartment-resolved human redox ratio is established. Human liver MPS data support directional zone functions under controlled 3-13% oxygen only.
All numeric rows are model predictions or a model setpoint; the only requested human comparator is NULL. Exact model hash, complete boundaries and a reproduced generation protocol are also absent.
Retained in the existing hepatic-flux evidence layer with original organ, splanchnic and systemic denominators.
Missing quantities remain explicit blockers and are not imputed.
Coverage visualization only; scientific records come from the primary sources and curated tables.
Derived visualization; its labels are superseded by the primary-source condition audit.
The plotted trajectories are model outputs without a human held-out comparator and cannot be called a held-out validation curve.

review_findings: A delivered contract is not a source of truth when it conflicts with reproduced local artifacts.
The Kemas PHH spheroid observations are valid same-format validation targets, not fresh-suspension or in-vivo single-hepatocyte parameters.
Hormone-bundle contrasts in Kemas cannot be interpreted as pure insulin effects because glucagon supplementation changes at the same time.
Wilson hepatocellularity supports a human organ-to-cell denominator but does not establish single-hepatocyte geometry.
No delivered numeric trajectory qualifies as independent held-out human validation.
No human in-situ zonal oxygen value is promoted into the simulation.

limitations: The validation targets are PHH 3D-spheroid net-consumption windows, not fresh-suspension production fluxes.
The exact exposure bundles include a glucagon difference, so high-versus-low insulin rows are not pure insulin interventions.
The human organ-to-cell conversion is contextual and cannot be applied to a per-kg-body-mass model output without a matched liver-mass bridge.
No exact-protocol model prediction or independent held-out human result is available.
observation_limitations: Net medium glucose disappearance is not a direct GLUT2 transport measurement and may combine uptake, utilization and production.
The denominator is seeded cells per 3D spheroid; viability and cell-number change are not a matched fresh-cell count at every interval.
The 0-72 h records overlap the three shorter windows and are not independent observations.
Only two source donors were used; table n=6 must not be interpreted as six donors.
healthy_phh_glucose_validation.policy: Preserve measured cell format, exposure bundle, time window, denominator and uncertainty. Do not convert a 3D spheroid net-consumption observation into a fresh-PHH production parameter or a receptor kinetic law.

limitations: The six batches are commercial cryopreserved PHHs, not a healthy in-vivo population reference interval.
Each endpoint is a mean plus SD for n=3 within-batch replicates; Table S4/S5 does not label the replicate type, and donor population variance is not estimated by those SD values.
Source-reported 0.0 values are interpreted as undetectable/censored, not as proof of zero enzyme activity.
Raw timepoints, LLOQs, covariance, viable-cell trajectories, donor genotypes and absolute CYP protein abundances are unavailable.
SCR and MFR are assay-level aggregate outputs and do not identify transport, binding, CYP turnover, POR coupling or metabolite-loss kinetics.

The CSCB criterion is stated for representative drug metabolism with CYP3A4/testosterone as the explicit example; it is not generalized here to every CYP and is not a physiological range or simulation pass threshold.

limitations: The measurement operator transforms a model output into the published observation space; it is not a biological rate law and supplies no parameter values.
The Kemas aggregate endpoint is a signed net medium balance and cannot identify bidirectional transport or intracellular pathway fluxes by itself.
The Grankvist isotope-tracing study demonstrates a measurement strategy in intact human liver tissue, not a transferable numeric calibration for PHH spheroids.
No synthetic trajectory, fitted parameter, acceptance threshold or model-state coupling is exported.

quantity_audit.reason: The assay directly constrains signed net glucose amount lost from the medium per seeded cell over each reported time window.
Net medium disappearance does not separate inward from outward glucose transport.
Donor-level net production proves that outward glucose flux can contribute, but the pooled medium endpoint does not quantify it separately.
Extracellular net glucose change does not measure intracellular glucose phosphorylation.
Medium glucose alone cannot resolve glucose-6-phosphate dephosphorylation from transport or other glucose-producing routes.
Net glucose disappearance does not report what fraction enters glycogen.
Net production can arise from glycogenolysis or gluconeogenesis and the current endpoint does not distinguish them.
Medium disappearance does not distinguish glycolysis from glycogen synthesis, pentose-phosphate flux or intracellular accumulation.
A signed net endpoint cannot allocate glucose production between gluconeogenesis and glycogenolysis.
No isotopologue or intracellular product measurement separates pentose-phosphate use from other glucose fates.
The supplement establishes a donor-dependent sign difference qualitatively, while Table 1 pools the healthy insulin-sensitive records and does not expose a numeric donor trajectory.
Low-insulin media also contain 100 nM glucagon, whereas glucagon in high-insulin media is not reported as a measured zero.
The reported denominator is seeded cells; window-specific viable-cell counts are unavailable.

limitations: Marker-positive FACS fractions and transcriptomic cell-type fractions are different measurement constructs and are not interchangeable purity estimates.
The six commercial PHH batches do not define a healthy in-vivo hepatocyte population distribution.
Non-hepatocyte fractions describe the composition of a PHH product; they are not organelles or state fractions inside one simulated hepatocyte.
Published cell-type percentages are rounded and may sum to 99.99 or 100.01 percent.
Five hepatocyte subsets were reported, but a machine-readable donor-by-subset numeric matrix is not curated here.
Six batches are insufficient for donor-disjoint generative-model training or causal donor-effect estimation.
The seven-donor proteome reports total protein-group copies per nucleus, not copies per cell or active molecules.
Cell-surface capture establishes identity but not membrane domain, orientation, density or active fraction.
BSEP and MRP2 kinetic records are recombinant vesicle assays with assay-protein denominators.
The two BSEP assays are independent contexts and must not be averaged into one pseudo-parameter.
The MRP2 values 183 and 104 are measured rates at 0.5 uM substrate, not reported Vmax values.
The NTCP-associated whole-hepatocyte uptake range is not an isolated-transporter Vmax and cryopreservation changes activity strongly.
The Bi 2006 taurocholate ranges are coupled sandwich-culture system outputs and cannot identify separate NTCP, OATP or BSEP rates.
The curated Bi 2006 record does not include individual-lot values, uncertainty statistics or the complete probe protocol needed for an exact model residual.
The INSR response observations do not identify receptor occupancy, binding kinetics or a dose-response law.
Cross-donor abundance variation is descriptive for seven surgical-resection donors and is not a healthy-population activity distribution.

surfaces.action: retain separate cytosolic, mitochondrial-intermembrane-space, mitochondrial-matrix, ER-lumen and peroxisomal pools plus source-supported transport/process topology; keep every unmeasured value and rate null
surfaces.limitations: The topology is not a numerical PHH model. Aggregate tissue values and total protein groups cannot initialize organelle states, local active concentrations, fluxes or kinetics.

surfaces.action: audit all nine legacy ATP/redox/OXPHOS reactions as placeholders; require matched compartments, observability, donor-disjoint validation and uncertainty before fitting or state coupling
surfaces.limitations: One apparent exchange measurement permits a future same-assay comparison only. It does not identify any of the nine legacy numerical reactions, and no current energy/redox parameter is fit-eligible or predictive.

surfaces.action: retain
surfaces.limitations: Systemic plasma target and whole-liver transit time do not resolve portal/arterial mixing or one sinusoid.

surfaces.action: retain categorical marker direction, measured protein gradients and controlled human-MPS oxygen context; block in-situ pO2 and numeric flux scaling
surfaces.limitations: Human atlas marker directions and normalized protein gradients are not donor-specific absolute expression or rate multipliers. The 3-13% oxygen values are controlled MPS settings, not in-situ human sinusoidal pO2 or reaction-rate effect sizes.

surfaces.action: retain checksummed human morphometry, surface identities, spatial protein gradients and ranked interaction hypotheses; prohibit cross-assay synthetic-donor fusion, 3D replacement, density or kinetic imputation, flux scaling and score-driven signaling
surfaces.limitations: The source layers come from different cohorts and assays. Two-dimensional area is not cell volume, cell-surface capture is not density or domain, normalized proteomics is not absolute abundance, and CellPhoneDB score is not binding probability or a kinetic rate.

surfaces.action: initialize the reference cell volume and aggregate healthy lipid-droplet display fraction; preserve all mesh, shape-distribution, domain-area, organelle-distribution and contact-ground-truth gates
surfaces.limitations: The source reports aggregate median/MAD summaries across five normal-control reconstructions, not raw individual-cell meshes. The 11278-cell analysis pools healthy and disease groups. The active 5657.07116 um3 median is not averaged with the conflicting 2850 um3 historical stereology estimate.

surfaces.action: retain perfusion relaxation; block blood-cell and zonal flux
surfaces.limitations: Whole-liver transit and systemic fasting glucose do not identify one sinusoid volume, GLUT2 exchange capacity or zone-specific cellular flux.

surfaces.action: retain measured trajectory; prohibit per-cell allocation
surfaces.limitations: Healthy-human mixed-meal glycogen and hepatic-output observations are whole-liver cohort averages, not single-cell or zone-resolved fluxes.

surfaces.action: use only for scale-matched validation; never initialize per-cell flux
surfaces.limitations: Thirty-one literature records span organ, splanchnic and whole-body scopes; portal-resolved healthy and single-hepatocyte flux are unavailable.

surfaces.action: select source-backed glycogen profile and preserve boundary missingness
surfaces.limitations: Organ flux observations have heterogeneous methods, units and scopes; they are retained separately and never averaged into per-cell rates.

surfaces.action: retain measured peripheral trajectory and causal clamp benchmark
surfaces.limitations: Mixed-meal hormones are peripheral plasma measurements; glycogen responses are liver-scale and do not identify receptor activity or per-cell kinetics.

surfaces.action: compare only exact measured point/window, unit and biological scale; do not interpolate or derive a pass threshold from SEM
surfaces.limitations: Study A liver glycogen and Study B endocrine/output observations use the same meal protocol but different participants; no cross-arm donor matching or mechanistic boundary activation is allowed.

surfaces.action: retain 16 measured mean-plus-SD windows and three insulin responses; compare only against a matched PHH 3D-spheroid protocol
surfaces.limitations: The PHH observations are net medium-glucose disappearance in two-donor 3D spheroids. Wilson hepatocellularity supports a contextual organ-to-cell denominator, not single-cell geometry or direct state initialization.

surfaces.action: retain source-locked method metadata, 12 non-overlapping cumulative-mean targets and four descriptive overlap audits; reject mismatched scale, unit, denominator, exposure or window matrices
surfaces.limitations: The paper does not identify the post-medium-change initial volume, remaining-volume schedule, volumetric factor, window-specific viable-cell count or covariance. Medium concentration reconstruction, pooled cumulative SD and pass/fail remain disabled.

surfaces.action: project only exact signed cumulative model outputs into the 16 protocol windows; keep nine mechanism-specific fluxes and all kinetic parameters unidentified
surfaces.limitations: The assay observes net medium glucose disappearance, not inward/outward transport or intracellular pathway allocation. Supplemental donor signs are qualitative, window-specific viable-cell counts are unavailable, and the isotope-tracing study supplies a future measurement design rather than transferable PHH numbers.

surfaces.action: retain the official 52-species, five-compartment, 36-reaction topology and canonical pool aliases; block numerical execution until runtime pool splits, duplicated export, lumping, compartment collapse and kinetic transfer are resolved
surfaces.limitations: The official publication supplement contains zero kinetic laws. Source-exact topology does not authorize fitted per-kilogram rates, and the exploratory runtime still splits cytosolic glucose and collapses cytosolic/mitochondrial cofactors.

surfaces.action: keep physiological sinusoid flow and finite PHH spheroid culture as separate contexts; accept only checksum-provenanced signed model fluxes on the exact 12 independent windows and derive the 16 assay windows without inventing volume or concentration trajectories
surfaces.limitations: Whole-liver transit does not identify one sinusoid. The PHH assay does not report challenge-medium volume, remaining-volume schedule, volumetric factor or window-specific viable-cell counts, so transport coupling and concentration reconstruction remain blocked.

surfaces.action: audit all 36 active reactions and all 16 PHH windows; permit exact-protocol descriptive residuals only; require mechanism-identifying measurements, donor-resolved trajectories, donor-disjoint splits, frozen artifacts and qualified uncertainty before fitting or activation
surfaces.limitations: The current endpoint identifies one aggregate medium balance and zero reaction-specific kinetic parameters. The 12 non-overlapping rows are same-format targets, while four 0-72 h rows overlap; none is a donor-held-out result.

surfaces.action: retain all six Table S3 24-hour PHH ELISA means and project only exact cumulative mature-albumin model outputs through the mass-conversion operator; assign no pass threshold
surfaces.limitations: The commercial 2D-PHH cohort is not an in-vivo reference population; Table S3 labels n=3 but not the replicate class, its cell-number denominator is not a window-specific viable-cell count, and the CSCB product criterion is not a simulation threshold.

surfaces.action: retain all 72 batch-resolved CYP SCR/MFR means; treat ten source-reported zero records as censored and compare only exact assay-matched model outputs without fitting or pass/fail
surfaces.limitations: Six commercial PHH batches with n=3 within-batch replicates of unspecified class do not define population variance. Raw concentration time courses, LLOQs, viable-cell trajectories, genotype and absolute CYP/POR abundances are unavailable, so transport and enzyme kinetics remain unidentified.

surfaces.action: retain five batch-resolved d8-TCA BEI values and apply the published paired-condition ratio only to exact sandwich-culture inputs; do not infer BSEP kinetics or assign a model pass
surfaces.limitations: Published BEI values combine uptake, intracellular handling, canalicular export, geometry and calcium-sensitive junction behavior. Paired A_Ca/A_CaFree values and uncertainty were not published.

surfaces.action: retain FACS marker fractions and scRNA-seq composition as separate assay surfaces; prohibit conversion of product composition into one-cell internal state or generative training
surfaces.limitations: Six commercial PHH batches do not define an in-vivo population distribution. Marker-positive fractions and transcriptomic cell-type fractions are different constructs, and the published five-subset count lacks a curated donor-by-subset numeric matrix.

surfaces.action: retain the article's rounded 600 pg/reference-cell and 8.7 billion molecule headlines while exposing the supplement's per-nucleus denominator; derive compartment protein masses by arithmetic only and prohibit geometry or dynamic proteostasis coupling
surfaces.limitations: The article uses a cell label while Supplementary Table 2 explicitly reports proteomic-ruler values per nucleus; they are not equivalent for binucleate hepatocytes. The 3000 um3 volume is derived from assumed 200 g/L protein, and compartment percentages are protein-mass fractions rather than geometry.

surfaces.action: retain all 8,689 quantified target protein groups and seven donor columns without imputation; permit explicit reference-nucleus population initialization but prohibit silent gene-group collapse, per-hepatocyte relabeling, surface localization, turnover or flux coupling
surfaces.limitations: The surgical-resection donors were not healthy volunteers. The workbook denominator is per nucleus, MaxQuant groups may contain multiple accessions or genes, and total abundance does not identify localization, active fraction or kinetics.

surfaces.action: retain direct seven-donor total BSEP and MRP2 observations per nucleus plus the independent MRP2 membrane-fraction context; block surface copies, active fraction, density, flux, and literal molecule rendering
surfaces.limitations: BSEP and MRP2 total copies are measured per nucleus, not per hepatocyte or canalicular surface. The independent MRP2 tissue fraction remains a separate denominator, and neither transporter has measured surface-localized or active copies.

surfaces.action: retain all vehicle and 10 uM troglitazone Table 4 means plus SD; preserve experiment-level BEI aggregation and prohibit healthy-state initialization or kinetic fitting
surfaces.limitations: Four-donor day-7 sandwich culture is not in-vivo physiology. Paired-buffer values use an estimated intracellular volume, source proxy zeros are censored observations, and aggregate BEI cannot be reconstructed from group means.

surfaces.action: require donor-resolved PHH synthesis plus compartment-resolved ER, Golgi, medium and degradation time courses before activating a constitutive secretion model
surfaces.limitations: The PHH ELISA endpoint identifies cumulative extracellular albumin only. Published HepG2 pulse-chase evidence supports pathway topology but cannot supply PHH translation, ER export, Golgi maturation, exocytosis or degradation rates.

surfaces.action: validate structure and provenance metadata, then curate each candidate against its primary source before parameter promotion
surfaces.limitations: A structurally valid external research bundle is not itself a curated parameter registry and cannot activate coupling automatically.

surfaces.action: retain audited predictions; prohibit cell-state coupling until all publication and scale-bridge gates pass
surfaces.limitations: The official supplement is non-kinetic; the permissively licensed current executable re-encoding reproduces two of five precision-derived publication benchmarks and represents mean liver behavior rather than a donor or zone-resolved hepatocyte.

surfaces.action: retain the exhaustive 36-reaction mapping and MathML fingerprints; activate no published parameter unless stoichiometry, compartment, symbolic law, per-cell units, biological context and validation all match
surfaces.limitations: Twelve active reactions have related candidates and three match stoichiometry after aliases, but none matches the full symbolic law, per-cell scale and healthy-PHH context. Fitted per-kilogram Vmax values cannot be copied into the single-cell network.

surfaces.action: retain the four-way protocol matrix; never substitute the unlicensed legacy executable for the vendored runtime
surfaces.limitations: The later author-repository lineage reproduces five reported targets under recovered conditions, but the repository protocol conflicts with the paper legend and exact publication-artifact equivalence remains unresolved.

surfaces.action: retain the unit-normalized HGO residual and 16 blocked PHH targets without assigning pass/fail; require matched dynamic context for validation
surfaces.limitations: The liver-scale model and human HGO estimate have unmatched time and boundaries. The PHH spheroid observations have no same-format model prediction, and the delivered model-only curve is not held-out human validation.

surfaces.action: retain source-backed pathway topology, experimentally observed PHH surface identities and the measured PHH insulin response; use geometry only to open the first gate; require local molecule density, orientation and 2D kinetics before binding; require a separate validated signal or transport law before state coupling
surfaces.limitations: Soluble hormones are exposure fields rather than collision bodies. Cell-surface capture supports SLC10A1, EGFR, INSR, MET, IL6ST, ABCB11, ABCC2 and ABCB1 identity but supplies no density, domain or orientation. A defined 1.7 uM insulin challenge has matched pAKT and PCK1/G6PC responses in PHH spheroids, but INSR surface abundance, receptor occupancy and complete kinetics remain unavailable. HBV preS1-NTCP attachment and EGFR-dependent internalization are separate gates; neither has project-ready PHH 2D kinetics.

surfaces.action: use engine-owned convex surfaces for signed gap, broad contact-patch geometry, membrane-domain labels, enter/stay/exit inputs and volume-preserving contact deformation; enforce the explicit 1% cross-system area-strain cap; do not use elapsed contact time as a causal parameter; keep force, adhesion, junction gating and biochemical activation blocked
surfaces.limitations: The canonical scale preserves the direct normal-control 3D median volume 5657.07116 um3; its 22.1071 um equivalent diameter is derived. The conflicting 2850 um3 historical stereology result and independent 18.4 um isolated-PHH median remain separate context checks. The regular truncated-octahedron boundary and affine deformation are kinematic proxies, not donor tissue morphometry or PHH rheology. One percent is an engineering cap equal to half the lower 2% human-RBC lysis bound. Computed patch area and deformation cannot identify adhesion bond density, force, stiffness, receptor occupancy or gap-junction permeability.

surfaces.action: require the pinned backend plus source-backed equations, complete units, calibrated parameters and geometry coupling
surfaces.limitations: Brian2 is an equation/event executor, not a source of hepatocyte biology. No calibrated communication model is attached.

surfaces.action: require donor-disjoint measured data, frozen schema, weight checksum, held-out evaluation and posterior predictive checks
surfaces.limitations: No audited donor-resolved training bundle or validated model artifact is loaded; synthetic candidates cannot drive the mechanistic engine.

surfaces.action: never use for quantitative validation
surfaces.limitations: All 48 pools are relative 0-1 placeholders.

surfaces.action: retain only as qualitative renderer driver
surfaces.limitations: Cycle rates, error fractions, damage and repair coefficients are uncalibrated.

surfaces.action: require a source-tagged calibration object
surfaces.limitations: Former per-hour base risks and stress weights were project assumptions.

surfaces.action: require measured context-specific probability
surfaces.limitations: Binucleated-cell prevalence cannot be used as a per-division failure probability.

surfaces.action: retain donor abundance, localization identity, assay kinetics, coupled whole-cell transport ranges and response timepoints as separate observables; require measured active surface copies and exact-protocol whole-cell predictions before coupling
surfaces.limitations: All eight selected proteins have seven-donor total abundance profiles. Six have PHH surface-identity evidence, three have a sourced physiological membrane domain, five transporter observations retain their original assay contexts, and three INSR-linked responses retain their measured timepoints. Bi 2006 adds five-lot coupled taurocholate apparent uptake, intrinsic biliary clearance and BEI ranges without assigning them to individual transporters. Surface copy count, active fraction, receptor binding kinetics, donor activity distributions and whole-cell protein rates remain unmeasured.

surfaces.action: require active surface copies and matched turnover
surfaces.limitations: Total or membrane-fraction abundance is not active canalicular surface abundance. Total-copy ratios cannot automatically scale functional activity.

surfaces.action: replace matched first-order ADP-to-ATP and ATP-to-ADP constants with compartment-resolved, mechanism-identified healthy-PHH kinetics
surfaces.limitations: The 29.5 mM/min whole-liver 31P-MRS result is apparent Pi-to-ATP exchange. It does not identify mitochondrial synthesis, ATP demand or either executable first-order constant.

surfaces.action: require compartment-resolved human GSH/GSSG/NADPH trajectories
surfaces.limitations: Legacy GPx, reductase, PPP and ROS influx rates are placeholders; isolation changes these pools.

surfaces.action: require matched PHH OCR, ATP-linked respiration, compartment states, membrane potential, active complexes and held-out validation
surfaces.limitations: P/O topology does not authorize the executable TCA/ETC Vmax, Km, seed pools or a single shared ATP/NAD(H) compartment.

surfaces.action: audit numerical parameter provenance separately from pathway topology; allow unsupported channels only in an explicitly exploratory runtime and fail closed for quantitative validation or prediction
surfaces.limitations: A pathway citation does not identify the numerical rate used by the runtime. Source-backed parameterization, matched biological/experimental context and independent held-out validation are separate gates.

surfaces.action: replace pseudo-first-order magnitudes with fitted human fluxes
surfaces.limitations: Topology and stoichiometry are useful, but several absolute rates are illustrative.

surfaces.action: require matched physiological exposure, receptor abundance/occupancy and dose-time response sufficient for a rate law
surfaces.limitations: The PHH spheroid pAKT and gene-expression responses close part of the chain, but the 1.7 uM challenge is a conventional culture concentration and no INSR abundance, EC50, Hill coefficient or portal-matched kinetics is available.

surfaces.action: do not claim calibrated time-to-death
surfaces.limitations: Relative stress thresholds are not matched human-hepatocyte commitment kinetics.

surfaces.action: run dynamics only for calibrated genes
surfaces.limitations: Reference coordinates are real; most donor expression and gene-specific kinetics are unknown.
